title: Flame_Transfer_Functions_for_Liquid-Fueled_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean_Direc
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None explicitly mentioned, but there's a reference to "fuel-modulation ratio, which is defined as the ratio of the root-mean-square (rms) to mean fuel line pressure

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
*   **Combustion Rig Optical Setup and Rotary Fuel Actuator:**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Rotary Fuel Actuator
        *   Radial Swirler
            *   Pressure
            *   Temperature
        *   ICCD
        *   Liquid Fuel
        *   Fuel Injector
        *   Preheated Air
        *   Needle Valve
        *   Fuel Pressure Measurement
        *   Dynamic and Static Pressure Ports
        *   Dump Plane
        *   Dynamic Pressure
        *   Choking Plate
        *   Quartz Tube
        *   To Exhaust
        *   Temperature
        *   Choking Plate
        *   Interference Filter Centered around 430
		*   PMT
        *   Motor Shaft
        *   Rotor (16 teeth equally spaced)
        *   Radial-Entry Swirler
        *   Fuel Inlet
		*   Fuel outlet

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirling air flow: 0.02 m upstream of the dump plane
*   Fuel nozzle: 2.5 GPH
*   Pressure drop across fuel nozzle: 689 kPa
*   Droplet Saunter Mean Diameter (SMD): less than 10 µm
*   PMT interference bandpass filter: ~430 nm or ~307 nm
*   FWHM: 10 nm
*   PMT placement: 0.06 m downstream and 0.56 m perpendicular to the quartz chamber
*   PMT viewing area: within 0.13 m downstream
*   N₂ Pressure: 1378 kPa
*   Rotary valve location: 0.46 m upstream of the fuel nozzle
*   Accumulator location: 0.2 m upstream of the rotary valve
*   Pressure transducer sensitivity: 172 kPa/V
*   Pressure transducer uncertainty: 0.25% of reading
*   Pressure transducer locations: 0.29 m and 0.45 m upstream of the fuel nozzle
*   Fuel-modulation amplitude can be greater than 30% up to 750 Hz and greater than 15% up to 1 kHz.
*   Dump Plane location: 1.25m
*   Distance after injector: 0.13m
*   Distance nozzle - quartz tube: 0.56m
*   Speed of sound for liquid fuel: typically above 1000 m/s

title: Flame_Transfer_Functions_for_Liquid-Fueled_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean_Direc
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here is the extracted technical content:

1.  Mathematical equations:
    *   m_f'(t)/m_f=Re(P'(0,t))/(2ΔP)
    *   Wm(s=iw) = CH*(s)/Pm(s)
    *   Wo(s=iw) = CH*(s)/Po(s)
 *  WN= (Ae^(-is(xw/c))+Be^(is(xw/c)))/(Ã+B) = (A/Be^(-is(xw/c))+(A/B)e^(is(xw/c)))/(A/B+1)

2.  Graphs
    *   **Top Graph:**
        *   Axes: Frequency (Hz), Amplitude
        *   Curves: Amplitude vs. Frequency (peaking around 750Hz)
    *   **Bottom Graph:**
        *   Axes: Frequency (Hz), Phase (Deg)
        *   Curves: Phase vs. Frequency

3.  Tables:
    *   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  Diagrams:
    *   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:
    *   Fuel flow rate: 1.4 g/s
    *   blocking ratio for the inlet and exit of the combustion chamber is 88% and 95%, respectively.
    *   sampling frequency: 5 kHz
    *   Data length: 8 s.
    *   The mean fuel pressure is 453 kPa
    *   the air flow rate is 66.7 g/s
    *   the equivalence ratio is 0.34
    *   the preheat temperature is 473 K
    *   fuel-modulation ratio is typically below 2.0%
    *   one-wave-mode acoustic frequency combustion chamber: 630 Hz
    *   combustion instability amplitude (without baffle plates, around 630 Hz): up to 12 kPa
    *   A/B = 1.80
    *   Magnitude of Wn: decreases from 1 at 0 Hz to 0.65 at 600 Hz
    *   Phase lead: increase from 0 at 0 Hz to 20.5 deg at 600 Hz
    *   SNR above 20 dB can be achieved with a fuel-modulation ratio larger than 0.5%.
    *   Even in the presence of self-excited large-amplitude combustion oscillations (up to 12 kPa in amplitude) around 640 Hz, the FTF at frequencies below 600 Hz is almost the same as those shown in Fig. 3.

title: Flame_Transfer_Functions_for_Liquid-Fueled_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean_Direc
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   W(z^-1) = CH'(z^-1) / P0(z^-1) = (-0.4213 z^-2 + 1.604 z^-3 - 1.147 z^-4) / (1 - 1.952 z^-1 + 1.525 z^-2 - 0.5222 z^-3)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3(a) Flame Transfer Function Wm(s):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Frequency (Hz), range 0-600
        *   y: Gain (no units given), range: 0-6.0
        *   y: Phase (Deg), range: -240 - 0
    *   Curves: Several curves plotted for different dates: 9/8/2008, 9/6/2008, 9/11/2008, 9/12/2008, 9/16/2008 (Jet-A), 9/17/2008 (a, Jet-A), 9/17/2008 (b, Jet-A)
*   **Fig. 3(b) Fuel-Modulation Ratio:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Frequency (Hz), range 0-600
        *   Fuel Modulation Ratio (no units given), range 0.00-0.30
    *   Curves: Three curves, for dates 09/17/2008 (Jet-A,a), 09/17/2008 (Jet-A, b), 9/16/2008
*   **Fig. 4 Flame Transfer Function Wo(s):**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: Frequency (Hz), range 200-600.
        *   y: Amplitude (no units), range 1.5-3.3.
        *   y: Phase(Deg), range: -180 - -30
    *   Curves: "Measurement" and "Model" on both figures.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   Mean fuel pressure: 453 kPa
*   Air flow rate: 66.7 g/s
*   Equivalence ratio: 0.34
*   Preheat temperature: 473 K
*   Fuel-modulation ratio: typically below 2.0%
*   Air flow rates: 58.4 g/s (phi=0.39), 66.7 g/s (phi=0.34), and 75.1 g/s (phi=0.30)
*   Fuel Flow Rate:1.4 g/s
*   Time Delay: 0.2 ms
*   Sampling Frequency: 5 kHz

title: Flame_Transfer_Functions_for_Liquid-Fueled_Swirl-Stabilized_Turbulent_Lean_Direc
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Da = tau / (tau_r + tau_v), where:

    *   Da is the Damköhler number
    *   tau is residence time
    *   tau_r is timescale of evaporation
    *   tau_v is timescale of chemical kinetics

**2. Graphs:**

*   **Figure 5: Flame transfer function W(s) at different air flow rates.**

    *   **Top:** Gain vs. Frequency (Hz)
        *   Curves:
            *   75.1 g/s
            *   66.7 g/s
            *   58.4 g/s
    *   **Bottom:** Phase (Degrees) vs. Frequency (Hz)
        *   Curves:
            *   75.1 g/s
            *   66.7 g/s
            *   58.4 g/s
        *   Key Values based on figure description: Fuel-modulation ratio typically below 2.0%, preheat temperature is 473 K, fuel flow rate is 1.4 g/s

*   **Figure 6: Flame transfer function W(s) at different preheat temperature.**

    *   **Top:** Gain vs. Frequency (Hz)
        *   Curves:
            *   473 K
            *   423 K
            *   373 K
    *   **Bottom:** Phase (Degrees) vs. Frequency (Hz)
        *   Curves:
            *   473 K
            *   423 K
            *   373 K
        *   Key Values based on figure description: Air flow rate is 66.7 g/s, equivalence ratio is 0.31

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel-modulation ratio: Below 2.0%
*   Fuel flow rate: 1.4 g/s
*   Air flow rate: 66.7 g/s
*   Preheat temperatures: 373 K, 423 K, 473 K
*   Equivalence ratio: 0.31
*   Air flow rates: 58.4 g/s, 66.7 g/s, 75.1 g/s
*   Ratio of H2/CO in syngas: 0.33 to 40.
*   Concentration of water vapor: 0% to 40%
* Frequency above 350 Hz.
* Frequency below 200 Hz.
